business_ideas.idea: Join Airbnb as employee with stock grants; obvious winner at $20B+ valuation
Work at OpenAI competing with Google, fastest-growing company ever
Join Retool for admin panel SaaS, 50-60% of enterprise software is internal-facing
Work at Mercury neo-bank for startups, replacing SVB position
Cursor IDE with AI, AI-assisted programmer tool competing with GitHub Copilot
Epirus defense company building EMP weapons to disable drones without bombs
Wiz cybersecurity for enterprise cloud, $350M ARR in 3 years
Perplexity AI search engine competing with Google via better accuracy
Neuralink brain-computer interface for paralyzed patients using brain-to-computer
Replit cloud-based IDE for 20M developers globally in 175 countries
Trava industrial worker staffing software for manufacturers

predictions.text: Sarah's list companies offer better risk-adjusted returns than founding or angel investing
Replit with 20M developers and AI tailwind could 5x from $1B valuation
Wiz could become $30B+ company replacing Silicon Valley Bank's market position

disagreements: Shaan skeptical of Cursor's PE multiples vs Sam who sees IDE potential; Shaan worried Neuralink 40hr requirement vs Sam who pitches it as relaxed opportunity; disagreement on whether high-growth startups are overpriced.
dynamics: Sam drives framework discussion of stock-grant investing as vehicle to wealth. Shaan adds tactical company analysis and founder psychology insights. Mix of serious pitch + comedic riffs (eggs story, dancing). Sam shows slides; both engage in competitive back-and-forth on valuations and founder quality. High engagement with tangents about culture, hiring, and CEO personalities.
frameworks: Sarah's List framework: join obvious winners post-product-market-fit with $200k stock package for low-risk 5x upside
Work as investment: treat job choice like VC investing your time and talent
Founder quality as differentiator: strong CEO correlates with startup success more than market size
The Gili Raanan model: leverage network effects and relationship capital for exponential growth
learnings: Obvious winners hidden in plain sight beat secret stock tips; visible dominance more reliable than contrarian picks
Revenue inflection points matter more than valuation; companies burning billions show actual business model
Founder origin stories reveal founder quality; immigrant founders often show unique problem-solving lens
Company culture selection trades personal wellbeing for wealth; 996 culture attracts ambitious people willing to sacrifice
Neo-banks prove market need but regulatory moat makes competitive threat real; FDIC insurance becomes product feature

banger_quotes.quote: The best place to invest is in your P&L; invest in companies you wouldn't cancel.
Perplexity is when you need the answer to be right. ChatGPT is when accuracy doesn't matter.
These are the cream of the crop of founders. We're talking Olympic athletes here.
Dream big. Be 1% in ambition, 1% in attitude, get 1% outcomes.

numbers_dropped: Airbnb: $20B+ valuation when Sarah joined, 1000+ employees
OpenAI: $103B valuation, $3.5B annual revenue, reached 100M users in 2-3 months
Retool: $3.7B valuation, $100M ARR in 2024 (from $500K first 9 months)
Mercury: $1.6B valuation, profitable, replacing $34B SVB position
Wiz: $12B valuation, $350M ARR in 3 years, $2B+ raised in single round
Replit: $1B valuation 3 years ago, now 20M developers (was 5M), scaling revenue
Stock package size: $200k over 4-5 years typical, 50k/year equity portion
notable: 2021 picks mostly underperformed (2 hits, 3-5 downs, pushes); 2024 picks betting market has corrected; Wiz story about Israeli intelligence mafia and CISO kickback model is dark/fascinating subplot about how business actually gets built
